title: Honoring Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc.®, on reaching the historic milestone of 117 years of serving communities.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
plain_summary: This congressional resolution celebrates Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority for serving communities for 117 years. The sorority was started in 1908 by nine women at Howard University. It has grown to have more than 120,000 active members around the world. The House of Representatives is congratulating the sorority for continuing to help communities through different programs and activities.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Education and Workforce.
subjects: Social work, volunteer service, charitable organizations

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution honors Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. for reaching the historic milestone of one hundred seventeen years of serving communities.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. was founded on January fifteenth, nineteen oh eight at Howard University in Washington, DC. Nine young women started this organization: Ethel Hedgemon, Anna Easter Brown, Beulah Elizabeth Burke, Lillie E. Burke, Marjorie Hill, Margaret Flagg, Lavinia Norman, Lucy Diggs Slowe, and Marie Woolfolk. These women built the sorority on five main goals: to encourage high academic and moral standards, to promote unity and friendship among college women, to study and help solve problems affecting girls and women to improve their status in society, to maintain an active interest in college life, and to provide service to all mankind. The sorority became officially incorporated on January twenty-ninth, nineteen thirteen. Since its founding in nineteen oh eight, Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. has grown to include over one thousand seventy-five chapters around the world, with more than one hundred twenty thousand active members. The organization has initiated more than three hundred sixty-five thousand members worldwide. The sorority is committed to running programs that help improve the well-being of local, national, and international communities. Their current service program theme for twenty twenty-two through twenty twenty-six is called Soaring to Greater Heights of Service and Sisterhood. This program includes six main areas of focus: Strengthen Our Sisterhood, Empower Our Families, Build Our Economic Wealth, Enhance Our Environment, Advocate for Social Justice, and Uplift Our Local Community. The sorority's current leadership uses these six areas to encourage members to take the lead in creating change, providing education, and speaking up for important causes. Among the many successful women who are members of this sorority is Vice President Kamala Harris, who is the first woman to serve as Vice President of the United States.

sections.title: Congressional Action
sections.text: The House of Representatives congratulates and praises Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc. for one hundred seventeen years of service.
